postTitle: FinOps and SRE Belong Together. I Built the Bridge.

short.text:
FinOps teams see the bill. SRE teams manage the resources. Neither has the full picture.

I call the bridge FinSRE. Cost as a reliability metric.

16,000 lines of PowerShell replaced with 200 lines of YAML. Open-sourced.

Where does the FinOps-to-SRE gap live in your organization?

medium.text:
FinOps teams see the bill. SRE teams manage the resources. Neither has the full picture.

I replaced 16,000 lines of PowerShell with 200 lines of YAML on the Azure SRE Agent platform. Six specialist subagents. AI-powered workload pattern detection. A FitScore system that validates whether a resize actually works, not just whether CPU is low.

I call the bridge FinSRE. Cost as a reliability metric, just like latency or availability.

I demoed it to the FinOps team at Microsoft. They went a different direction. I open-sourced it anyway.

FinOps without operations is just a dashboard. SRE without cost visibility is just firefighting. Where does that gap live in your organization?
